steps.title: Identify what the example must show
steps.content: The claim is that elevation and prominence can differ significantly. The example needs a single mountain with both values shown so the reader can see the gap between them.

steps.title: Match the data
steps.content:
Nanda Devi's elevation is $7{,}816$ m and its prominence is only $3{,}139$ m — a gap of more than $4{,}600$ m, exactly the kind of large difference the claim describes.

An entry that gives only elevation does not show the contrast, and comparisons between different mountains do not compare elevation and prominence for one mountain.

That is the correct answer.
